answer:
\boxed{voicing contrast}

Eastern Armenian is one of the few languages that distinguishes between voiced and voiceless consonants (e.g., /d/ vs. /t/, /g/ vs. /k/) **only in word-final positions**, where other languages often neutralize these distinctions. This feature is rare because most languages resolve voicing contrasts earlier in the word. The contrast is phonemic, meaning it changes word meaning (e.g., *մեր* [mer] "our" vs. *մեր* [mɛr] "me").
